title: Boat Rental in Hurghada: Costs, Tips and Best Routes for 2026
html:
Hurghada, on the Red Sea, offers world-class boating from marinas such as the Marina Hurghada and the modern Port Ghalib Marine. Prices for a half-day motorboat charter start from around 3,500 EGP. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Hurghada or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Hurghada?

Half-day motorboat hire starts from about 3,500 EGP and full-day catamaran trips from 8,000 EGP. Prices vary by season, boat size and duration. For an accurate overview, you can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24 to see current rates. You can compare verified boat listings in a few minutes.

Where to set off in Hurghada

Departure points are spread along the coast. Marina Hurghada is the main hub, close to restaurants and parking. Port Ghalib Marine, about 50 km south, is a newer marina with full facilities. For day trips to Giftun Island, many operators launch from the jetty near Sheraton Road or from the small harbour at El Gouna, 35 km north. The private marina at Sahl Hasheesh is also an option for chartering a vessel directly from a resort. One option worth a look is the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP for hire.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor Boats, Best for short excursions and island hopping; up to 8 guests; from 4,000 EGP for four hours. A popular model is the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP for hire, ideal for snorkelling trips to Giftun Island.
  2. Sailing Yachts, Perfect for sunset cruises and leisurely sailing; up to 6 guests; from 5,000 EGP for half a day.
  3. Catamarans, Spacious for groups and families; up to 12 guests; from 8,000 EGP for a full day with lunch included.
  4. Luxury Yachts, For a premium experience with crew; up to 20 guests; from 15,000 EGP per day. Models include the Azimut 50 and the Fairline Squadron.
  5. Rigid Inflatables (RIBs), Fast and agile for watersports and fishing; up to 6 guests; from 2,500 EGP per hour.

When to go and do you need a licence?

The best season is from March to November, with peak months June to October when the wind is steady and water temperatures exceed 25 °C. No licence is required for private charters with a skipper, but bareboat hire may need an International Certificate of Competence (ICC), operators confirm exact requirements.

Book trips early in the morning (8, 9 am) to avoid afternoon winds, especially on the way to Giftun Island, where the sea can get choppy by noon.
If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

Costs at a glance

  • Price range: 3,500, 20,000 EGP per half-day
  • Peak months: June to October
  • Trip lengths: 4, 6 or 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: 8, 12 on most boats
  • Currency: Egyptian Pound (EGP)

At a glance

  • Best for: Snorkelling, sunbathing and island tours
  • Popular destinations: Giftun Island, Mahmya Beach, Orange Bay
  • Distance: 25 nautical miles from Hurghada to Giftun Island
  • Boat types: Motor boats, catamarans, yachts

Questions about boat rental in Hurghada

Can I get a refund if the weather is bad?

Most operators offer a full refund or reschedule if the trip is cancelled due to unsafe conditions.

What is included in the charter price?

Typically includes skipper, fuel, life vests, snorkelling gear and sometimes lunch; check the listing for details.

Do I need to pay a deposit?

Many listings require a deposit of 20, 30% to confirm the booking, paid online via BoatHire24.

How far in advance should I book for summer 2026?

Booking at least two weeks ahead is recommended, especially for large groups or luxury yachts.

To find the right boat, use the boat search on BoatHire24 to filter by date, budget and boat type. Whether you want a quick trip to Giftun Island or a full-day catamaran cruise, the platform makes it easy. If you own a boat in Hurghada, you can rent out your boat on BoatHire24 and earn extra income. The platform handles bookings securely.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Hurghada and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
